title: 208 People Killed in US Drug Boat Strikes — No Evidence Required
script: 208 dead — for what? The US military struck another alleged drug boat in the eastern Pacific on Tuesday — 1 person killed, 2 survivors pulled out. That brings the total to 208 people killed in these strikes since September, a campaign the Trump administration calls a war on narcoterrorists. Here's the part the Pentagon is quieter about: the military did not provide evidence that Tuesday's boat was carrying drugs. It was in a known smuggling route. That's the standard. You're in the zone, you get targeted. Democratic lawmakers and legal scholars have raised alarms. 208 deaths. No drug evidence required. No trial. Just a strike in the Pacific and 2 survivors to tell about it. So who exactly is deciding what counts as a narcoterrorist boat out there?
